channel.title: we are fenris on Fenris
channel.description: Recent content in we are fenris on Fenris

item.title: Summer 2025: Midyear Momentum
item.description: As summer unfolds, we’re seeing the value of sustained engagement. Supporting leaders, navigating hype cycles, and helping systems take root. Here’s a look at what’s been happening across the Fenris network in a year that has presented so many challenges to US civil society.

item.title: 2024 in Review: Reflecting & Refocusing
item.description: Fenris has celebrated growth and opportunities in recent months, and collaborated with clients working on climate change, waste management, and food justice. As 2024 winds down, though, we are taking time to reflect not just on what we’ve accomplished, but on our mission. We are motivated to deepen our commitment to building a better, more equitable world.

item.title: The First Data Leadership Cohort Wraps Up
item.description: Digital Public and Aspiration, the Data Leadership Cohort (DLC) is an opportunity for peer learning for governance, stewardship and long-term responsible leadership in data-centric missions and ventures. Our first cohort launches in the first quarter of 2022.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Fenris is committed to protecting the privacy of visitors to our website. This privacy policy describes how this site collects and manages information about you, and how we use that information.

We hope this policy is clear and understandable. It is based on principles and templates provided by the digital impact toolkit and ToS;DR.

When you visit or view gofenris.com, the site collects no personally identifiable information about you.

We may use cookies for the purposes of web analytics and server logs. Web analytics and server logs are hosted on our own servers and related data are not shared with any third party services. Logs are purged periodically, approximately once a quarter.

item.title: The Data Leadership Cohort: Call for Participation
item.description: Co-organized by Fenris, Digital Public and Aspiration, the Data Leadership Cohort (DLC) is an opportunity for peer learning for governance, stewardship and long-term responsible leadership in data-centric missions and ventures. Our first cohort launches in the first quarter of 2022.

item.title: How We Work
item.description:
1. We are a values-driven consultant network. We care about who we work with and how. We care about the impact our work and our clients have in the world.

  2. Our clients are the experts. Your organization and its stakeholders understand the problem space better than we ever will. We listen and learn before we propose, and center your team’s excellence.

  3. We build for the long term. Governance, ownership, and sustainability are built into every engagement, not added later when something breaks.

item.title: Manifesto
item.description:
Fenris is a values-driven network. The work we do is inseparable from what we believe about data, power, and who gets to benefit from both.

This is our attempt to say that clearly. We invite you to read it in the same spirit it’s written: as a working document, not a final word. We’ll get some of it wrong. We expect to revise it.

01 We work with teams we believe in.

We work with mission-driven teams seeking meaningful positive impact on the world, first and foremost. We are excited to work with on issues related to climate change, social justice, and more equitable outcomes. In many countries, including the USA, this means working with civil society organizations. We believe civil society needs a greater quality of strategic thinking, technical excellence, and governance expertise than is found in the private sector.

item.title: Who We Are
item.description:
Founded by Aman Ahuja, Fenris is a network of independent practitioners who help civil society organizations use data and AI technologies effectively.

We are technologists, designers, data governance experts, and product strategists — senior practitioners who have chosen to focus their expertise where it is hardest and most needed.

We are small by design. Each member brings deep experience and works as a peer, not a subcontractor. We assemble the right team for each engagement, drawing from the network as the work demands.
